program: Business English — Executive Communication for International Leadership
hub_subtitle: Business English Executivo para Reuniões Internacionais e Liderança

lesson.menu_desc: Making recommendations in the Thursday meeting -- modals of advice: should, ought to, had better, I would recommend -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- What Do You Recommend? -- Making Recommendations in the Meeting
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Izabel Cristina de Pieri | Aula 5 -- What Do You Recommend?

listenings.text: Hi Izabel, this is Victor, your operations consultant, with a few recommendations before Thursday. First, I would strongly recommend automating the monthly report. You should not be preparing those numbers by hand anymore, so let us make that the top priority. Second, I think you ought to delegate the supplier follow-ups to your team, so that you can focus on strategy. On costs, I would advise holding off on any new hire until the audit is done. You had better wait for those figures first. And one more thing: it might be worth outsourcing the payroll, because it takes far too long in-house. Weigh the options with Andy, and let me know what you decide. Talk on Thursday.

Good morning, Izabel. This is Claire, from the board, with a quick note before the call. First, congratulations on the progress this year. It has been remarkable. Now, a few suggestions from the board. We think you should prioritize the reporting system, because it is the one thing still slowing you down. We would also recommend that you draft a simple twelve-month plan, so the partners can see where AVRT is going. On the new market, our advice is to reconsider the timing. You might want to postpone the launch until the second half of the year. And please do not try to do everything at once. You had better focus on two or three priorities. We will discuss all of this on Thursday. Thank you.
